title: volume not chowned before container start
body:
The volume option U does not seem to result in the volume being recursively chowned prior to entrypoint on the first run.

Example: This specific container checks permissions of the mounted dir in the container and fails if they are set to root.

https://github.com/abraunegg/onedrive
```
[root@fedora volumes]# podman run --name test -v newvolume:/mount:U,Z --user 1000:1000 onedrive:latest
```

Although the volume is created it is not chowned before the entrypoint is called and it throws an error. You can confirm the permissions on the host will show as `root:root`.  

```
[root@fedora volumes]# ls -lthd newvolume/_data/
drwx------. 3 root root 4.0K Mar  10 02:25 _data

[root@fedora volumes]# podman rm test
```

Now remove the container and recreate it without recreating the volume. The volume is successfully chowned this time around. The `U` flag is skipped/ignored if the volume is created during a `podman run` for some reason, I am not sure if there is something specific this container is doing or if it can be reproduced with others easily. I did try creating the volume before hand with `podman volume create` but experienced the same behaviour where only after the second run of the container do the permissions get set correctly prior to entrypoint call.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Please fill out the entire template when opening an issue. Knowing which versions of Podman in which environment is run is oftentimes incremental to debug.  Can you share the output of `podman info --debug`?

I cannot reproduce on my end.

I also cannot reproduce it.

I am closing the issue as it lacks feedback, please reopen if you've more data to share

Hi - I also experienced the same issue and had some trouble pinpointing what was causing this behaviour in the particular script where it appeared, but eventually was able to narrow it down to having a `buildah run` statement after `buildah config --volume=[..]`. Not sure if there are other requirements but I was able to consistently trigger/avoid the issue.

See below a minimum example that triggers the behaviour by setting an env variable:

```
#!/bin/bash

set -eux -o pipefail

# always create a new volume
podman volume rm testing || :

BASE="alpine"
FROM=$(buildah from $BASE)

mkentrypoint() {
cat <<EOF
#!/bin/sh
set -eux
ls -la /srv/testing/
touch /srv/testing/test && echo 😎 || echo 💥
EOF
}

mkentrypoint | buildah run $FROM -- \
    /bin/sh -c 'cat > /myentrypoint.sh && chmod 755 myentrypoint.sh'

buildah config \
    --user=nobody \
    --volume=/srv/testing \
    --cmd='["/myentrypoint.sh"]' \
    $FROM

# running this script with `env KABOOM=1` will trigger a failure to access the
# newly-created volume due to incorrect permissions

[[ ${KABOOM:-} = 1 ]] && buildah run $FROM -- \
    /bin/sh -c "set -x && ls -la /srv/testing"

buildah commit $FROM testing:latest

podman run --rm -it \
    --volume testing:/srv/testing:U \
    testing
```

Regarding environment:
```
> podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.1
API Version:  4.0.1
Go Version:   go1.17.8
Git Commit:   c8b9a2e3ec3630e9172499e15205c11b823c8107
Built:        Sun Mar 13 07:44:23 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

I was able to replicate it on two machines, running either rootless or as root.

+ [[ '' = 1 ]]
+ buildah commit alpine-working-container testing:latest

total 0

😎

Seems to work with podman 4.0.3.

@rhatdan You are not setting the environment variable.

Run it like this:

`env KABOOM=1 sh /tmp/t.sh`

I just repeated the test with podman 4.0.3 and the issue is still there.

This seems like more of a buildah bug then Podman.

$ podman run -v newvolume:/mount:U,Z --user 1000:1000 ubi8 ls -ld /mount
drwxr-xr-x. 2 1000 1000 6 May  5 14:08 /mount

Just repeated the test with buildah 1.28.0 + podman 4.3.0 and it's still happening.

```
Version:         1.28.0
Go Version:      go1.19.1
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.23.0
Git Commit:      79f41e3235ecc17d711a9aa06ec769234ec95399
Built:           Tue Oct  4 02:34:51 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

```
> podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b-dirty
Built:        Wed Oct 19 17:09:30 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```
For the record: adding ",Z" to the bind mount options doesn't make any difference.
What command exactly blew up, can you generate a simple repeater?

Does NOT work:

```
FROM=$(buildah from alpine)

buildah config \
    --user=nobody \
    --volume=/srv/testing \
    --cmd='["ls","-la","/srv/testing"]' \
    $FROM

buildah run $FROM /bin/sh -c "/bin/true"

buildah commit $FROM testing:latest

podman run --rm -it \
    --volume testing:/srv/testing:U \
    testing
```

Does work:

```
FROM=$(buildah from alpine)

buildah config \
    --user=nobody \
    --volume=/srv/testing \
    --cmd='["ls","-la","/srv/testing"]' \
    $FROM

buildah commit $FROM testing:latest

podman run --rm -it \
    --volume testing:/srv/testing:U \
    testing
```

There is literally a one-line difference between them (the `buildah run` line before `buildah commit`).

This is a trimmed-down version of the same script I pasted above, with the niceties removed (cleanup, logs, etc).

The first one works for me with 
```
$ cat /tmp/t.sh 
FROM=$(buildah from alpine)

buildah config \
    --user=nobody \
    --volume=/srv/testing \
    --cmd='["ls","-la","/srv/testing"]' \
    $FROM

buildah run $FROM /bin/sh -c "/bin/true"

buildah commit $FROM testing:latest
$ sh /tmp/t.sh

Copying blob e5e13b0c77cb skipped: already exists

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
4c355e100cb28e0ad60f515b8ef3ee37dc927d46415aecc49bc43083c00bb6b6
$ podman run --rm -it \
    --volume testing:/srv/testing:U \
    testing
total 0
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root             6 Nov 21 19:28 .
drwxr-xr-x    1 root     root            21 Nov 21 19:28 ..
$ podman -v
podman version 4.3.1
```

Unfortunately no, it is not working for you as well. The permissions are incorrect.

Please check the title of this issue.

Running the first script (NOT OK):

```
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root            60 Dec 24 00:18 .
drwxr-xr-x    1 root     root            60 Dec 24 00:18 ..
```

Running the second script (all good, permissions are updated):

```
drwxr-xr-x    2 nobody   nobody          60 Dec 24 00:18 .
drwxr-xr-x    1 root     root            60 Dec 24 00:18 ..
```

Keep in mind you need to remove the temporary volume (testing) between execution of both scripts.

See my previous comment from **Nov 20** for both scripts.

I come from the future to say I can no longer reproduce the issue on my machine:

```
# buildah version
Version:         1.42.1
Go Version:      go1.25.8
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.2.1
CNI Spec:        1.1.0
libcni Version:  v1.3.0
image Version:   5.38.0
```

```
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.7.0
API Version:  5.7.0
Go Version:   go1.25.8
``` 

Just to confirm, I re-tested with an older installation and I was able to reproduce the issue with these versions:

``` 
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.7.2
API Version:  4.7.2
Go Version:   go1.21.3
```

```
# buildah version
Version:         1.32.2
Go Version:      go1.21.3
Image Spec:      1.1.0-rc.4
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.28.0
```

So it was likely fixed between these releases.
thanks for confirming it!
